curriculum_description.en: A comprehensive journey through the Arabic language, following CEFR standards with a focus on classical and modern standard Arabic.
curriculum_description.ar: A comprehensive journey through the Arabic language, following CEFR standards with a focus on classical and modern standard Arabic.
curriculum_description.fr: A comprehensive journey through the Arabic language, following CEFR standards with a focus on classical and modern standard Arabic.
curriculum_description.de: A comprehensive journey through the Arabic language, following CEFR standards with a focus on classical and modern standard Arabic.
curriculum_description.ru: A comprehensive journey through the Arabic language, following CEFR standards with a focus on classical and modern standard Arabic.
curriculum_description.zh: A comprehensive journey through the Arabic language, following CEFR standards with a focus on classical and modern standard Arabic.

unit_description.en: Master the Arabic alphabet, basic sounds, and initial word construction.
unit_description.ar: Master the Arabic alphabet, basic sounds, and initial word construction.
unit_description.fr: Master the Arabic alphabet, basic sounds, and initial word construction.
unit_description.de: Master the Arabic alphabet, basic sounds, and initial word construction.
unit_description.ru: Master the Arabic alphabet, basic sounds, and initial word construction.
unit_description.zh: Master the Arabic alphabet, basic sounds, and initial word construction.

grammar: Short Vowels: Fatha (a), Kasra (i), Damma (u). Sukun represents no vowel.

reading.text: أَبِي فِي الْبَيْتِ. الْأَرْنَبُ يَأْكُلُ التَّمْرَ.

question.en: What sound does 'Ba' with a Fatha make?
question.ar: What sound does 'Ba' with a Fatha make?
question.fr: What sound does 'Ba' with a Fatha make?
question.de: What sound does 'Ba' with a Fatha make?
question.ru: What sound does 'Ba' with a Fatha make?
question.zh: What sound does 'Ba' with a Fatha make?

grammar: Connectivity: Letters change shape at the beginning, middle, and end of a word.

reading.text: الْجَمَلُ فِي الْجَبَلِ. مَعِي حَلِيبٌ وَخُبْزٌ.

grammar: Non-connectors: Dal, Dhal, Ra, Zay, Alif, Waw do not connect to the following letter.

reading.text: الرَّجُلُ مَعَهُ ذَهَبٌ. هُنَا دَارٌ وَزَهْرَةٌ.

grammar: Shadda: Doubling a consonant sound (e.g., Say-ya-rah). Emphatic letters: Sad, Dad, Ta, Za.

reading.text: السَّمَكُ فِي الْمَاءِ. الشَّمْسُ تُعْطِي ضَوْءًا.

grammar: Ayn and Ghayn are unique pharyngeal sounds. Solar letters assimilate the 'L' of 'Al-'.

question.en: Does Ghayn sound like a French R?
question.ar: Does Ghayn sound like a French R?
question.fr: Does Ghayn sound like a French R?
question.de: Does Ghayn sound like a French R?
question.ru: Does Ghayn sound like a French R?
question.zh: Does Ghayn sound like a French R?

lesson_objectives: Identify Fa, Qaf, Kaf, Lam, Meem, Nun, Heh, Waw, Yeh.

grammar: Long Vowels: Alif (aa), Waw (uu), Yeh (ee). These extend the short vowel sounds significantly.

reading.text: مَعِي قَلَمٌ وَكِتَابٌ. الْوَلَدُ يَشْرَبُ الْمَاءَ.

unit_description.en: Learn to introduce yourself, your family, and talk about your origins.
unit_description.ar: Learn to introduce yourself, your family, and talk about your origins.
unit_description.fr: Learn to introduce yourself, your family, and talk about your origins.
unit_description.de: Learn to introduce yourself, your family, and talk about your origins.
unit_description.ru: Learn to introduce yourself, your family, and talk about your origins.
unit_description.zh: Learn to introduce yourself, your family, and talk about your origins.

grammar: Response structure: Sabahu al-Nur response to Sabahu al-Khayr. Alaykumu al-Salam response to As-Salamu alaykum.

reading.text: أَهْلًا وَسَهْلًا. كَيْفَ الْحَال؟ أَنَا بِخَيْر، الْحَمْدُ لله.

question.en: What is the response to 'As-salamu alaykum'?
question.ar: What is the response to 'As-salamu alaykum'?
question.fr: What is the response to 'As-salamu alaykum'?
question.de: What is the response to 'As-salamu alaykum'?
question.ru: What is the response to 'As-salamu alaykum'?
question.zh: What is the response to 'As-salamu alaykum'?

grammar: Arabic Pronouns: No verb 'to be' in present tense. 'Ana Ahmad' means 'I am Ahmad'.

reading.text: اِسْمِي أَحْمَد. أَنَا مِنْ مِصْر. وَأَنْتَ، مِنْ أَيْنَ أَنْتَ؟

question.en: Read: اِسْمِي أَحْمَد. أَنَا مِنْ مِصْر.
question.ar: Read: اِسْمِي أَحْمَد. أَنَا مِنْ مِصْر.
question.fr: Read: اِسْمِي أَحْمَد. أَنَا مِنْ مِصْر.
question.de: Read: اِسْمِي أَحْمَد. أَنَا مِنْ مِصْر.
question.ru: Read: اِسْمِي أَحْمَد. أَنَا مِنْ مِصْر.
question.zh: Read: اِسْمِي أَحْمَد. أَنَا مِنْ مِصْر.

grammar: Possession: Add 'yee' for my (Ummi), 'uka' for your-m (Abooka), 'uki' for your-f (Abouki).

reading.text: هَذِهِ أُمِّي. هَذَا أَخِي. اِسْمُ أَبِي أَحْمَد.

grammar: Li is used for personal attributes or family. Indi is used for physical objects. Indi Kitab (I have a book).

reading.text: عِنْدِي كِتَابٌ وَقَلَمٌ. لِي أَخٌ وَاحِدٌ.

grammar: Nisbah: Add 'iyyun' (ّي) for male and 'iyyah' (يّة) for female to the country name to form a nationality.

reading.text: أَنَا مِصْرِيٌّ. هُوَ أَمْرِيكِيٌّ. هِيَ فَرَنْسِيَّةٌ.

question.en: How to make a feminine nationality?
question.ar: How to make a feminine nationality?
question.fr: How to make a feminine nationality?
question.de: How to make a feminine nationality?
question.ru: How to make a feminine nationality?
question.zh: How to make a feminine nationality?

lesson_objectives: Review complete family and nationality sets.

grammar: Review: Pronouns (Ana, Anta, Anti, Huwa, Hiya), Possession markers, and Nationality Nisbah. You are now identity-complete in Arabic!

reading.text: أَنَا طَالِبٌ فِي الْجَامِعَة. صَدِيقِي مُهَنْدِسٌ سَعِيدٌ.

question.en: Translate: My friend is a student
question.ar: Translate: My friend is a student
question.fr: Translate: My friend is a student
question.de: Translate: My friend is a student
question.ru: Translate: My friend is a student
question.zh: Translate: My friend is a student
